text: The maximal lactate steady state workload (MLSSw) is closely related to endurance sport performance and the average marathon velocity is just below MLSSw.
At the maximal lactate steady state intensity, carbohydrates contribute more than 50% of total energy needs and fuel utilization crosses over from predominantly fat to predominantly carbohydrate.
The blood lactate concentration at maximal lactate steady state varies greatly between individuals, ranging from 2 to 8 mmol/L in capillary blood, and is not related to the workload at MLSS.
Enhanced lactate clearance in trained individuals is attributed primarily to oxidation in active muscle and gluconeogenesis in the liver.
Endurance training increases the expression of MCT1 monocarboxylate transporters, which facilitate lactate transport into and out of cells, with variable effects on MCT4.
Lactate exchange and removal capacity, estimated via blood lactate recovery velocity constants, is related to time to exhaustion at VO2max.
